data.title: Google's AI-generated weather forecasts blow away traditional models
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) revealed an artificial intelligence-powered weather forecast model called GenCast today that blows away traditional forecasting models in accuracy.

The model produces 15-day forecasts faster and more accurate than other weather prediction systems, including those by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts. In a study released in Nature, GenCast was more accurate than the ECMWF model 97.2% of the time and 99.8% if lead times were greater than 36 hours.

"An ensemble forecast expresses uncertainty by making multiple predictions that represent different possible scenarios," according to a blog post today by Google DeepMind. "If most predictions show a cyclone hitting the same area, uncertainty is low. But if they predict different locations, uncertainty is higher. GenCast strikes the right balance, avoiding both overstating or understating its confidence in its forecasts."

GenCast also produces forecasts much faster than more traditional weather models. For example, a single Google Cloud TPU v5 can produce a 15-day forecast in eight minutes while simultaneously generating many more. By comparison, traditional physics-based ensemble forecasts would take hours on a supercomputer.

Google said improved forecasting is beneficial for agriculture as well as predicting behavior for extreme weather events, such as cyclones, typhoons and hurricanes.

"We are eager to engage with the wider weather community, including academic researchers, meteorologists, data scientists, renewable energy companies, and organizations focused on food security and disaster response," Google said.

data.title: U.S. senators, agencies to hold secret meeting on "Salt Typhoon" hack by China - Reuters
Relmada plunges 77% as phase 3 depression candidate unlikely to meet primary endpoint
data.description:
• Relmada Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RLMD) plunged ~77% in Wednesday afternoon trading after announcing that an interim analysis of a phase 3 trial for depression asset REL-1017 is not likely to meet its primary efficacy endpoint.
  • The company said a Data Monitoring Committee found that that the Reliance II study is futile. The study is evaluating REL-1017 as an adjunctive treatment for major depressive disorder in combination with an antidepressant.
  • The pharma said it will evaluate next steps for the candidate, adding that it will now focus on a phase 1 study of REL-P11, a candidate for metabolic disease.
  • Relmada noted that it had $54.1M in cash and cash equivalents as of Sept. 30.

data.title: Trump nominates Atkins to succeed Gensler as SEC chair
data.description:
President-elect Donald Trump nominated Paul Atkins, a former commissioner of the Securities and Exchange Commission, to become the next SEC chair, he said on Wednesday.

"Paul is a proven leader for common sense regulations. He believes in the promise of robust, innovative capital markets that are responsive to the needs of Investors, & that provide capital to make our Economy the best in the World," Trump wrote on a post on Truth Social.

Atkins is likely to ease current SEC Chair Gary Gensler's strict stance on digital assets, like cryptocurrencies. "He also recognizes that digital assets & other innovations are crucial to Making America Greater than Ever Before," Trump said about Atkins.

Crypto prices appear to be mixed in Wednesday afternoon trading. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose 0.3% to ~$95.8K, ethereum (ETH-USD) jumped 6;9% to $3.83K, cardano (ADA-USD) -2.1%, XRP (XRP-USD) -3.9%, solana (SOL-USD) +1.5%, and dogecoin (DOGE-USD) +1.1%.

Last week, Bloomberg had reported that Atkins was a front-runnner for the SEC chair.

Most bitcoin-related stocks are in the green. Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) rose 4.2%, MicroStrategy (MSTR) +1.0%, MARA Holdings (MARA) +0.2%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) +2.0%, Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) +7.5%, BIT Mining (BTCM) +6.8%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Why you should be checking Reddit again ahead of trading
data.description:
One of the key dynamics in supporting a year-end stock rally is retail investors, Goldman Sachs’ technical specialist Scott Rubner said in underscoring his keeping a close eye on trending names among that market cohort.

In the dwindling number of trading days left in 2024, the S&P 500 (SP500) on Wednesday was on course to log its 56th record closing high. It has also climbed in 10 of the past 11 trading sessions through Tuesday. The overall advance is in tune with Rubner’s late-November call that a rally will push the S&P 500 (SP500) to 6,200 by the end of 2024.

Retail traders are “home for the holidays” and “unite at the town watering hole,” as they participate in a so-called year-end “FOMO” rally, Rubner said in a Wednesday note. “I am back to checking Reddit every morning to see the names of the day,” he said, referring to trending stocks and options on the social media website.

In Wednesday’s Daily Discussion thread on Reddit's (RDDT) WallStreetBets, Nvidia (NVDA), Intuitive Machines (LUNR), and Archer Aviation (ACHR) were among the top names, along with the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY). WallStreetBets was thrust into prominence during the height of the meme-stock craze in 2021, with GameStop (GME), AMC (AMC), and Chewy (CHWY) among favorite names with retail investors.

Goldman Sachs’ Equity Panic Index, a rolling percentile of four equity volatility metrics, has wiped out new lows “and we are seeing retail activity to pick up,” Rubner said. “This can also been seen in record call options (largest on record) and high-flying names,” he said, and published this chart:

The Reddit (RDDT) ticker in Wednesday trade was among the top of AltIndex’s essentially real-time list of stocks being discussed in investing subreddits. (AAPL), (PLTR), (TSLA), (AMZN) and (MSTR) rounded out the top 10 trending tickers.

Other S&P 500 (SP500) investors can track include: (VOO), (IVV), (SH), (RSP), and (UPRO).

data.title: Trump picks Gail Slater as head of Justice Department's antitrust unit
data.description:
President-elect Donald Trump picked Gail Slater, a policy adviser to Vice President-elect JD Vance, to be in charge of the Dept. of Justice's antitrust department.

"Big Tech has run wild for years, stifling competition in our most innovative sector and, as we all know, using its market power to crack down on the rights of so many Americans, as well as those of Little Tech!" Trump said in a post on Truth Social. "I was proud to fight these abuses in my First Term, and our Department of Justice’s antitrust team will continue that work under Gail’s leadership."

Slater would replace Jonathan Kanter as the top dog at the DOJ's antitrust division.

Slater worked as a tech policy adviser on the National Economic during Trump's first term. Previously, she worked at the Federal Trade Commission for 10 years, including as an adviser to former Democratic FTC Commissioner Julie Brill.

The Finacial Times reported last Wednesday that Trump was looking at Slater for the job and described her as a tough enforcer.

"In her new role, Gail will help ensure that our competition laws are enforced, both vigorously and FAIRLY, with clear rules that facilitate, rather than stifle, the ingenuity of our greatest companies," Trump wrote in the post on Wednesday.

More on Trump's administration picks

  • Trump names Peter Navarro as senior trade adviser
  • Donald Trump picks Kevin Hassett to head National Economic Council
  • Trump taps Jamieson Greer as U.S. trade representative
  • Trump picks Howard Lutnick to lead Commerce Department

data.title: Maersk cut to Sell equivalent at Morgan Stanley on shipping oversupply concerns
data.description:
A.P. Moller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) -3% in European trading Wednesday as Morgan Stanley downgrades shares to Underweight from Equal Weight, as concerns about oversupply in the shipping market have re-emerged after the boost to shipping rates from attacks on Red Sea vessels by Houthi rebells fades.

"Negative earnings risks make Maersk a value trap," Morgan Stanley analyst Cedar Ekblom writes. "We see container supply growth materially outpacing demand."

The shipping sector is weighed by excess capacity after it invested in fleets during the COVID-19 disruption, and new tariffs threatened by President-elect Trump are seen potentially curbing demand.

Morgan Stanley says its downgrade reflects Maersk's (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) high operational gearing, indicating that minor changes in net income can lead to substantial variations in earnings and free cash flow projections.

Also, J.P. Morgan analysts cut their stock price target on Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) to 8,450 kroner from 9,000 kroner, suggesting a 33% decrease from Tuesday's close.

data.title: Experian announces acquisition of Audigent
data.description:
• Experian (OTCQX:EXPGY) has acquired Audigent, a data activation and identity platform in the advertising industry.
  • With the addition of Audigent’s combined first-party publisher data and inventory network, Experian adds sell-side distribution to its existing demand-side capabilities and builds on its identity activation technology.
  • EXPGY shares were up 1.1% on Wednesday.

data.title: BTIG is bullish on APP, FLUT, DKNG, and RBLX as it places Buy ratings on all four
data.description:
BTIG’s Jonathan Krinsky is bullish on AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP), Flutter Entertainment (NYSE:FLUT), DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG), and Roblox Corporation (NYSE:RBLX), as he tagged Buy ratings on each of the stocks pointing out strong technical and fundamental pictures for the stocks.

Regarding price targets, Krinsky gave APP a target of $432, FLUT was given a target of $312, while DKNG and RBLX were at $55 and $56, respectively. Moreover, see what the analyst had to specifically say about each of the four names below:

AppLovin Corporation (APP):

“One of the strongest charts in the entire market, up over 800% YTD. At this point for investors looking to add exposure, we would look for some consolidation. That could be through time, or price. Even the short-term 20 DMA is down at $296, or ~18% lower. We suspect price will either retrace towards that, or it will consolidate sideways and allow that 20 DMA to catch up.”

  • Currently APP trades at $370.85 a share and is +830.6% year-to-date.

Flutter Entertainment (FLUT):

“After several years of consolidation, the stock broke out of a range in September. This breakout carries an upside measured objective towards $360. Look for dips into the $250-260 range to provide good support as an entry point for new purchases.”

  • Currently FLUT trades at $278.28 a share and is +55.4% year-to-date.

DraftKings (DKNG):

“A strong run into early 2024 resulted in consolidation for much of this year. It has now put in a new base in the 35-45 range, which suggests an upside objective towards $55 on a breakout. There should now be good support in the $40-41 range should a pullback develop.”

  • Currently DKNG trades at $44.67 a share and is +26.7% year-to-date.

Roblox Corporation (RBLX):

The stock has spent much of the last three years in a well defined trading range between $25 and $50. It broke out above that $50 level on a high volume gap in late October, and has thus far held that gap. This suggests it was a breakaway gap, and therefore doesn't need to get filled before resuming higher. It now looks poised to trend higher into '25, with a measured upside objective of $75.

  • Currently RBLX trades at $53.96 a share and is +18% year-to-date.

data.title: CMS rolls out new payment model for Vertex, bluebird gene therapies
data.description:
Bluebird bio (NASDAQ:BLUE) and Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) have entered into outcome-based agreements with the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services aimed at expanding patient access to their respective gene therapies for sickle cell disease, or SCD.

The agreements are part of a new voluntary CMS payment model called the Cell and Gene Therapy Access Model. Under the agreements, payments for the treatments for Medicaid patients will be tied to whether the therapies improve health outcomes, according to a CMS statement.

CMS said it will now engage with states and U.S. territories participating in the Medicaid Drug Rebate Program to help them determine whether they want to participate in the model. States can begin participating in the program between January 2025 and January 2026.

The agency noted that approximately 50% to 60% of Americans with SCD are enrolled in Medicaid. It added that SCD-related hospitalizations and health episodes cost the system around $3B annually.

Bluebird bio (BLUE) and Vertex (VRTX) both market one-time gene therapies to treat the disease. Bluebird’s (BLUE) treatment is marketed under the name Lyfgenia, while Vertex markets its therapy, which was co-developed with CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP), under the name Casgevy.

Casgevy’s list price is reportedly $2.2M, while Lyfgenia lists at $3.1M.
